mapping.a_element: bidirectional coupling between real and virtual pendulums
mapping.b_element: feedback loops in the NF-kB core network driving oscillations
periodic spiking in nuclear NF-kB concentration

mapping.a_element: critical condition (sufficiently close lengths) for synchronization
mapping.b_element: network design enabling oscillatory behavior in NF-kB

brief_justification: While both abstracts describe coupled oscillatory systems with critical conditions for emergent behavior, paper A's mechanism is based on instantaneous bidirectional coupling leading to synchronization, whereas paper B's mechanism involves a biochemical network generating periodic spikes through feedback loops and binding kinetics. The underlying mathematics (coupled oscillators vs. biochemical rate equations) and driving processes differ fundamentally.
